circles.title: Understanding Knowledge Access Through Elmentor Circles
circles.intro: To structure access, engagement, and leadership within the community, we use a model called Elmentor Circles. These circles define different levels of participation and responsibility, arranged in clear layers:
circles.conclusion: This model helps us structure engagement, learning, and decision-making in a clear and scalable way.

audience.name: Audience
audience.description: External audience who engage with us from the outside

members.name: Members
members.description: Active, permanent participants in the community

gas.name: Group Advisory (GAs)
gas.description: Leaders who support and guide members

lgas.name: Lead Group Advisory (LGAs)
lgas.description: Guide both the Group Advisory and members

executives.name: Executives
executives.description: Responsible for all levels, ensuring the overall direction and success of the community.
